page: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g-2880s is an x86_64 architecture server within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 48 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, with 48 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is comprised of a 2880 GB NVMe SSD, and the server is provisioned with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression and decompression utilities scoring in the top 10%. Multi-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. The memory subsystem exhibits a top-tier write speed for large blocks, though large-block read-only memory bandwidth and overall PassMark memory scores fall into the average tier.

The shared CPU allocation model presents a potential performance tradeoff under sustained, concurrent multi-tenant workloads, despite the high physical core count. However, the combination of 192.0 GB of RAM and 2880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and storage-heavy operations. This server is suited for database operations, static web serving, and text generation tasks using small to medium language models, where it demonstrates top-tier inference speeds.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g-2880s is an x86_64 server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 48 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 192.0 GB of memory, and 2880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While the shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, the server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and large-block memory write speeds. Multi-core processing and database operations rank in the top 25% of benchmarks, though large-block uncached memory reads remain average. This resource profile provides high storage and memory density, making it qualitatively cost-effective for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. It is suited for database hosting, static web serving, and small to medium scale LLM text generation.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g-2880s is a VX1 Cloud Compute server with 48 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 2880 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong database and single-core performance.

2880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-capacity local data access
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and large-block memory write speeds
Strong multi-core performance, database operations, and static web serving
Suited for database hosting, static web serving, and LLM text generation
